text: Age-predicted maximal heart rate is more accurately estimated by the equation 208 - 0.7 × age than the traditional 220 - age formula.
Age alone explains most of the variance in maximal heart rate, with a strong inverse correlation (r = -0.90).
Maximal heart rate is independent of gender — the regression line for predicting HRmax does not differ between men and women.
Maximal heart rate is independent of habitual physical activity level — wide variations in activity status do not influence the HRmax prediction equation.
population: healthy adults including endurance-trained and sedentary

text: The traditional 220 - age formula underestimates maximal heart rate in older adults, leading to underestimation of exercise intensity during testing and inappropriate exercise prescription.

Heart rate training zones prescribed using the 220 - age formula may set inappropriately low intensity targets for masters athletes, since their true HRmax is likely higher than predicted by that equation.
